description: PT5/lacO is the bacteriophage-T5 sigma-70 promoter flanked by two lac operators, giving lacI-repressible, IPTG-inducible transcription (the pQE/Bujard expression module).

references.authors: Bujard H, Gentz R, Lanzer M, Stueber D, Mueller M, Ibrahimi I, Haeuptle MT, Dobberstein B
references.title: A T5 promoter-based transcription-translation system for the analysis of proteins in vitro and in vivo
Promoters recognized by Escherichia coli RNA polymerase selected by function: highly efficient promoters from bacteriophage T5
The Nucleotide Sequence of the lac Operator

references.authors: Simons A, Tils D, von Wilcken-Bergmann B, Mueller-Hill B
references.title: Possible ideal lac operator: Escherichia coli lac operator-like sequences from eukaryotic genomes lack the central G X C pair

references.authors: Besse M, von Wilcken-Bergmann B, Mueller-Hill B
references.title: Synthetic lac operator mediates repression through lac repressor when introduced upstream and downstream from the lac promoter

label: PT5/lacO
note: PT5/lacO is the bacteriophage-T5 sigma-70 promoter flanked by two lac operators, giving lacI-repressible, IPTG-inducible transcription (the pQE/Bujard expression module).
Sigma-70 -35 hexamer of the bacteriophage T5 promoter (seq[18:24]=TTGCTT).
Sigma-70 -10 hexamer, perfect match to the TATAAT consensus (seq[41:47]=TATAAT), 17 bp downstream of the -35 box (canonical spacer).

label: lac operator
note: lac repressor (lacI) binding site, core TGTGAGCGGATAACAAT (seq[24:41]).

label: lac operator (downstream)
note: Second lac repressor (lacI) binding site, core TGTGAGCGGATAACAAT (seq[56:73]), positioned 3' of the transcription start.
